project.name: Emerald Grove Veterinary Clinic
project.description: Spring Boot 4.0.0 petclinic app — Thymeleaf, Spring Data JPA, H2/PostgreSQL
project.tests: JUnit 5 unit tests + Playwright E2E tests in e2e-tests/
project.docker: Dockerfile at repo root, must build for linux/amd64 platform (ECS requirement)

secrets.secret: must be set after OIDC role is recreated via Terraform

aws.state: ALL infrastructure has been destroyed. Everything must be recreated from scratch.

modules.module: Account-level OIDC identity provider and GitHub Actions IAM role. Must be applied FIRST before any workflows can authenticate. After apply, copy the output role ARN into GitHub secret AWS_ROLE_ARN.
All staging environment infrastructure. Applied via the init-infrastructure GitHub Actions workflow or locally.

workflows.required_permissions: contents: read id-token: write # Required for OIDC — must be present in every workflow
workflows.ecr_login_step: - name: Log in to Amazon ECR id: login-ecr run: | aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | \ docker login --username AWS \ --password-stdin 277802554323.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com echo "registry=277802554323.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

deploy_workflow.trigger: push to main + pull_request (opened, synchronize, reopened)
deploy_workflow.steps: 1. Checkout (fetch-depth: 0, fetch-tags: true) 2. Configure AWS credentials (OIDC) 3. Set up JDK 17 (temurin, maven cache) 4. Run unit tests: ./mvnw test 5. Set up Docker Buildx 6. Log in to ECR 7. Determine image version (git tag or sha-SHORT_SHA) 8. Build and push Docker image (linux/amd64, versioned + latest tags) 9. Update ECS service: a. Get current task definition ARN (save for rollback) b. Fetch task definition JSON c. Update image in containerDefinitions[0] d. Strip read-only fields (taskDefinitionArn, revision, status, requiresAttributes, compatibilities, registeredAt, registeredBy) e. Register new task definition revision f. Update ECS service to new task definition 10. Wait for ECS to stabilize: aws ecs wait services-stable 11. Get ALB DNS: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --names petclinic-staging-alb-mumford 12. Health check loop: poll /actuator/health up to 30x with 10s sleep, expect HTTP 200 and JSON {"status":"UP"} 13. Set up Node 20 (npm cache, cache-dependency-path: e2e-tests/package-lock.json) 14. npm ci in e2e-tests/ 15. Install Playwright chromium only: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium 16. Run E2E tests with BASE_URL=http://{ALB_DNS} and CI=true 17. Upload E2E test results artifact (always, even on failure) 18. Rollback on failure: update ECS service back to saved task definition ARN + wait for stabilization 19. Deployment summary (always): print status, URL, image tag

init_workflow.steps: 1. Validate environment name (non-empty, matches ^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$) 2. Checkout code 3. Verify terraform/{environment}/ directory exists 4. Configure AWS credentials (OIDC) 5. Set up Terraform 1.9.0 6. terraform init (working-directory: terraform/{environment}) 7. terraform plan -out=tfplan 8. terraform apply -auto-approve tfplan 9. Capture outputs: alb_dns_name, ecr_repository_name, ecs_cluster_name, ecs_service_name 10. Set up JDK 17 11. Build app jar: ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests 12. Set up Docker Buildx 13. Log in to ECR 14. Build and push initial image as v1.0.0 + latest (linux/amd64) 15. sleep 30 then aws ecs wait services-stable 16. Health check loop (same as deploy workflow) 17. Infrastructure summary (always): print all endpoints and resource names

destroy_workflow.trigger: workflow_dispatch with inputs: - environment (string, required) - confirmation_word (string, must equal "DESTROY" exactly) - acknowledge_deletion (boolean, must be true)
destroy_workflow.steps: 1. Validate all three inputs (fail fast, before checkout or AWS calls) 2. Print destruction warning listing all resource types, sleep 10 3. Checkout code 4. Verify terraform/{environment}/ directory exists 5. Configure AWS credentials (OIDC) 6. List ECR images (continue-on-error: true) 7. Batch delete all ECR images (continue-on-error: true) 8. Set up Terraform 1.9.0 9. terraform init 10. terraform destroy -auto-approve 11. Verify ECS cluster deleted (continue-on-error: true) 12. Verify ECR repository deleted (continue-on-error: true) 13. Verify terraform show reports empty state 14. Destruction summary (always)

context.order_of_operations: To get everything working from scratch: 1. Fix the four known Terraform bugs listed above 2. aws sso login 3. cd terraform/github-oidc && terraform init && terraform apply 4. Copy the output role ARN into GitHub secret: AWS_ROLE_ARN 5. Trigger "Initialize Infrastructure" workflow in GitHub Actions UI 6. Push to main to trigger the deploy workflow
